header.label: Socioeconomic Analytics
header.headline: OBC leads at 44% — General has the lowest gender parity of all categories
header.sub: 26.44 Cr students across OBC, General, SC, and ST categories. SC+ST together form 28.5% of enrollment but their share peaks at Preparatory (29.8%) and drops 3.4 points by Secondary — signalling a structural access gap

cards.label: OBC
cards.note: Largest social category — 44.3% of total enrollment across all education levels

cards.label: General
cards.note: Lowest GPI (0.916) of all four categories. Girls underrepresented despite higher private school access

cards.label: SC
cards.note: GPI improves to 0.945 at Secondary level — girls who remain in school persist at higher rates

cards.label: ST
cards.note: Highest GPI of all categories. Secondary GPI reaches 0.988 — girls outpace boys who remain

2020-21.chartGpiNote: GPI = 1.0 is exact parity. General (0.916) has the widest gender gap. At Secondary level all categories exceed 1.0 — girls who persist outpace boys.
2020-21.chartGpiSource: Girls outnumber boys in OBC and SC categories at the GPI level — but ST communities show a persistent gap, indicating barriers to girls' schooling in tribal regions.
2020-21.chartLevelNote: SC+ST share rises to peak at Preparatory (29.8%), then drops to Secondary (26.4%) — a 3.4-point fall indicating disproportionate dropout at the Secondary transition.
2020-21.chartLevelSource: SC and ST enrollment concentration drops sharply at Secondary and Higher Secondary — the drop-off begins at the transition from Upper Primary, where distance and cost barriers peak.

insight1.label: SC + ST Combined Enrollment Share
insight1.delta2_label: SC+ST share drop from Preparatory (29.8%) to Secondary (26.4%)

insight2.label: General Category GPI — Lowest of All Groups
insight2.delta1_label: General GPI at Secondary — recovers to near-parity for those who persist
insight2.delta2_label: SC Secondary GPI — girls outpace boys at the highest measured level
